**Information for Chem 91r, 98r, 99r**  
[**https://tinyurl.com/chem-research-for-credit**](https://tinyurl.com/chem-research-for-credit) 

Last updated: March 5, 2026

This site is for students who would like to enroll in **CHEM 91R (first year or sophomore), CHEM 98R (juniors),** or **CHEM 99R (seniors)** research course. To be eligible to enroll, you must have a Harvard-affiliated principal investigator agree to mentor you for the semester. If you plan to take Chem 91r/98r/99r for your *5th* course, you can wait till the add/drop period to petition to enroll. 

### Note that you cannot do research for credit and for pay at the same time per Harvard college policy. If you are enrolled in Chem 91r/98r/99r, you cannot receive pay for the same research work during the semester. 

Starting in the Fall 2025 term, students can petition to fulfill the Advanced Laboratory requirement with two courses of CHEM 91R/98R/99R (i.e., Research-for-Credit courses) taken for **two consecutive semesters** doing **chemical research**. Students who select this option are required to submit a **scientific technical report** at the conclusion of each semester, which must receive a satisfactory evaluation from the Principal Investigator (PI). [Click here for](https://drive.google.com/file/d/125AuEIX8BuTk7OnFeX2VRYjkdOFEMOgi/view?usp=sharing)  the Guideline and Petition Form for more details.

| Expectations for students doing research and earning credit |  |
| ----- | :---- |
| The workload averages about **10 hours** of research-related work each week for the term. For equity to all enrollees regardless when they enroll in the course, students are expected to complete 130 hours in total by the end of semester (13 weeks of work, 10 hours per week on average). Students must complete basic laboratory safety training and other safety training required by the host lab prior to starting work. Students must always follow the safety guidelines and training provided by the lab/department.   No written work is required for this credit bearing research. You do not need to formally "log" anything for this course, but you should meet with your direct lab mentor in the lab and have a rough plan for when you will do your work each week and make them aware if you need to change when you are coming in.   **Grading.** This “class” is not letter-graded; students will earn either a Satisfactory or Unsatisfactory grade. The Office of Undergraduate Studies in Chemistry will keep in touch with your PI, and we will send your PI a request for a recommended grade for you before the end of the semester.  |  |
| **How to document your PI’s approval for you to enroll in 91r/98r/99r** |  |
| To be eligible to enroll, you must have a Harvard-affiliated principal investigator\* agree to mentor you for the semester.  For this reason, students must reach out to labs ahead of the start of the semester and obtain permission from the professor to do research for credit in their lab.  \*Note: if you plan to count Chem 91r, 98r, or 99r toward your concurrent master’s degree in chemistry and your PI is not in the [Department of Chemistry and Chemical Biology](https://chemistry.harvard.edu/faculty),  please check with [Prof. Ted Betley](https://chemistry.harvard.edu/people/theodore-betley), the Director of Graduate Studies, if you have not done so.   Starting 2024 fall, we have new procedures to document your PI’s approval for you to enroll in 91r/98r/99r. **Please make sure to complete all the steps below, which are necessary steps for enrolling in the course because your PI will be the person who provides us your grade in the course. Discuss with your PI:** Communicate with your PI for their approval to work in their lab for a research-for-credit course; it’s helpful to share with them the course expectations listed above. If they do approve, please inform your PI that they will receive an email from [chemistrydus@fas.harvard.edu](mailto:chemistrydus@fas.harvard.edu) with instructions to officially approve your participation in their lab\*\*, and it only takes a few clicks to confirm.  **Petition on my.harvard:** Submit a petition to enroll in the course on my.harvard.edu, and we will approve within 2 business days. **Enroll on my.harvard:** after we approve your petition, please enroll in the course officially on my.harvard.edu as soon as possible. **Fill the Lab-PI Form:** only after you officially enroll in the course can we email you the Lab-PI Form for you to fill out (HarvardKey required). You will fill in the information regarding your PI, your direct mentor (a graduate student or a post-doc) and the lab admin if applicable \-- filling out the direct mentor and lab admin is especially helpful if your PI might not be able to respond to emails and can delegate the approval. **You can search their name by entering their Harvard email address.** The Lab-PI Form will be emailed to you within 2 business days after you officially enroll in the course.   After you fill the Lab-PI Form, we will contact your PI for their official approval of your participation in our system. So the sooner you complete Steps 1-4, the sooner we can email your PI. After your PI officially approves via our system, your enrollment is then complete. **Students who fail to obtain the official PI’s permission by the add/drop deadline (the latest is the 5th Monday) will have to drop the course.**    |  |
| **Contact: Office of Undergraduate Studies in Chemistry** |  |
|                  ![][image1] **Director of Undergraduate Studies: [Dr. Gregg Tucci](https://chemistry.harvard.edu/people/gregory-tucci)** [tucci@fas.harvard.edu](mailto:tucci@fas.harvard.edu) |                * ![][image2]* **Assistant Director of Undergraduate Studies: [Dr. Lu Wang](https://chemistry.harvard.edu/people/lu-wang)**  [wang29@fas.harvard.edu](mailto:wang29@fas.harvard.edu) |
